programmes.show_description: ABC: Accompanied by the Southbank Sinfonia at the London Palladium, pop band ABC perform and discuss The Lexicon of Love, one of the 1980s' most successful debut albums. (S1, ep 3)
This documentary tracks the history of Queen's legendary reign, from their humble beginnings and meteoric rise to their rightful place as the Champions of the World. (1995)
The Courtauld Institute Of Art: A series of fascinating shorts taking you deeper into the world of art and the people behind it.
Andi Oliver and Elizabeth Day host as members of Good Housekeeping magazine's book club meet Sebastian Faulks. Plus, Monique Roffey and Bernardine Evaristo. (5 of 6)
There's a bidding frenzy in New York as the work of Jean-Michel Basquiat goes up for auction, with his painting 'Untitled' set to break records. (S8, ep 12)
This special episode heads to Sotheby's where the late David Bowie's art collection, which includes works by Damien Hirst and Jean-Michel Basquiat, goes under the hammer. (S7, ep 11)

programmes.show_name: Michael Flatley Lord Of The Dance: Dangerous Games
programmes.show_description: Choreographed by Michael Flatley, 40 of the world's most talented performers combine the best of traditional and contemporary music and dance.
In this art competition, amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 41)
In this art competition, amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 42)
There's One Born Every Minute: Arthur and Margaret are a quiet couple. When Margaret inherits £50,000, Arthur reluctantly agrees to gamble it all on a dubious property deal. (S4, ep 13)
Bosom Friends: Nell and Emma are old friends who are reunited after years apart. However, things turn sour when the penniless Emma looks to drag Nell down into poverty with her. (S4, ep 14)
One More Mile to Go: Sam Jacoby has his wife's corpse in the trunk of his car, and is menaced by a motorcycle cop, who nags him about a taillight. (S2, ep 28)
Vicious Circle: A hired killer reluctantly sets out to kill his own girlfriend as ordered by his crime boss, knowing he won't accept any failures. (S2, ep 29)
A look at the life and career of the acclaimed Hollywood director, whose films include Stand by Me, When Harry Met Sally... and the Oscar-nominated A Few Good Men. (S6, ep 8)
A look at the life of the Academy Award-winning Hollywood icon, famous for her roles in Pretty Woman, Erin Brockovich, My Best Friend's Wedding and Notting Hill. (S11, ep 9)
Glowing Future: Jack and Betsy are lovers. That is until Jack decides to go halfway across the world to be with his new Australian lover. But Betsy isn't going to make it easy. (S4, ep 15)
Way To Do It: The casino offers Roger an escape from his domineering aunt. He has a love for both gambling and a pretty hostess. But the casino boss has a score to settle. (S4, ep 16)
An exploration of Himid's life over seven days after the crew departs, as Kate captures candid moments with just an iPhone.
National treasure Dame Judi Dench invites her friend, Sir Kenneth Branagh, for tea at her Surrey home. They swap star-studded stories from their careers on stage and screen.
A look at the career of the Academy Award-nominated director, known for his gripping depictions of life stories and characters - such as his acclaimed film The Queen. (S6, ep 9)
He's a household name who starred in Pretty Woman and the Academy Award-winning musical Chicago. This is a look at his life and career. (S11, ep 10)
Hijack: A hijacker on an aeroplane takes a stewardess hostage before demanding a million dollars and a parachute. Will Captain Waterhouse give in to his demands? (S4, ep 17)
Blue Marigold: Dropped by her agency for her diva-like behaviour, supermodel Marigold's mental health deteriorates. Years later, she plots a comeback. (S5, ep 1)
The Three Dreams of Mr Findlater: Ernest Findlater is constantly nagged at by his wife so he fantasizes about Lalage, an exotic lover from the South Seas. (S2, ep 30)
The Night the World Ended: When a newsman shows a homeless man a fake article that says the world is going to end that night, his prank has deadly consequences. (S2, ep 31)
A look at the life of the Academy Award-winning actor, known for his roles in Alfred Hitchcock's The 39 Steps and as Mr Chipping in Goodbye, Mr Chips. (S12, ep 10)
...Of Terminator 2: Judgment Day. Movie critic Ian Nathan examines what made this 1991 sci-fi-action sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, a classic of the genre. (S1, ep 6)
A loving tribute to the Hollywood superstar known for his roles in blockbuster hits like Dirty Dancing, Ghost and The Outsiders. With insight from Demi Moore, Rob Lowe and others.
The story of Academy Award-winning director Spike Lee, who wowed audiences with gripping films like Malcolm X, Inside Man and BlacKkKlansman. (S6, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: Kate Winslet: A Quest For Authenticity
programmes.show_description: The reasoning behind Kate Winslet's choice of roles. Throughout her career, she has chosen to play roles of authentic women with complex psyches and realistic bodies.

programmes.show_name: Blue Road: The Edna O'Brien Story
programmes.show_description: Edna O'Brien, 93, reflects on her life, novels and love through journals read by Jessie Buckley, with insight from writers like Gabriel Byrne.

programmes.show_name: National Treasures - The Art Of Collecting
programmes.show_description: In Venice we explore the late Francesco Carraro's remarkable collection of Venetian glass, which includes works by the much-lauded Carlo Scarpa. (S1, ep 2)
The huge personal guitar collection of Pink Floyd's David Gilmour comes to Christie's - including the iconic black Fender Stratocaster which featured on many of the rock legend's hits.
New. David Hockney's Portrait of an Artist (Pool with Two Figures) is a modern masterpiece and now it's up for sale at Christie's in New York with an estimate of $80 million.
Get ready to relive the magic of an unforgettable evening with André Rieu. Recorded live during his spectacular Summer Open Air Concerts in Maastricht 2024.

A divorcée falls for an over-the-hill cowboy who is struggling to maintain his romantically independent lifestyle.
The last episode of the series takes a deep dive into the life and career of John Carpenter, known for his cult classics such as Dark Star, The Thing and Halloween. (S6, ep 10)
A look at the life and career of filmmaker behind Easy Rider, his acclaimed directional debut. He also starred in Apocalypse Now, Super Mario Bros and Blue Velvet. (S11, ep 11)
The Eavesdropper: Moira hears a woman discussing a trip to Paris with her lover Donald. Not only is her husband called Donald, but he was in France last week. Coincidence? (S5, ep 2)
Operation Safecrack: In an attempt to prove their safes are impregnable, a company challenges ex-safebreaker Sam to crack one for a cash prize. But are his skills sharp enough? (S5, ep 3)
The Hands of Mr Ottermole: A murderer has been strangling people in the London fog. A reporter manages to continually make it to the crime scene before the police. (S2, ep 32)
A Man Greatly Beloved: In a small Massachusetts town, precocious young Hildegarde Fell learns a secret about crusty old John Anderson. (S2, ep 33)
Britpop: Elbow frontman Guy Garvey lifts the lid on two decades of TV gold. Here, he takes you through music genre and culture movement Britpop. (5 of 5)
A look back at the historic Jimi Hendrix concert performance at the Fillmore East, New York on 1 January, 1970.
Highlights from Jimi Hendrix's iconic 1969 performance at Woodstock, featuring Purple Haze, Voodoo Chile, Hear My Train A Comin' and Star Spangled Banner.

programmes.show_name: Phil Lynott: Songs For While I'm Away
programmes.show_description: The story of how a Phil Lynott, young black boy from working-class 1950s Dublin, became Ireland's Greatest Rock Star.
